data.title: DEADLINE NEXT WEEK: Berger Montague Advises Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. (NASDAQ: RARE) Investors to Contact the Firm Before April 6, 2026
data.description: In Philadelphia on April 3, 2026, Berger Montague PC, a prominent plaintiffs' law firm, disclosed the initiation of a class action lawsuit against Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. The legal action is being taken on behalf of investors who acquired common shares of the Company. Ultragenyx, trading under the ticker symbol RARE on the NASDAQ, is the subject of this lawsuit.

data.title: Solo miner connected to CKPool receives $210K Bitcoin block reward, 1 of only 20 solo‑mined blocks in past year, amid miners selling BTC.
data.description: A solo miner connected to CKPool recently received a significant $210,000 Bitcoin block reward. This is noteworthy as it is only the 20th solo-mined block in the last year. At the same time, other listed miners are selling their BTC holdings in order to maintain their financial stability.

data.title: Dmail to stop services on May 15 due to expensive infrastructure, unsuccessful fundraising, and low token usage.
data.description: On May 15, the Dmail Network will be closing down due to high infrastructure expenses, unsuccessful fundraising efforts, and limited token utility.

data.title: CobbleStone® Announces Masterclass, Two Weeks Away: AI‑Driven, Agentic Contract Management Excellence
data.description: On April 17, 2026, CobbleStone Software will be conducting a live masterclass from 9:00 AM to 11:45 AM ET in Princeton, N.J. The event aims to showcase the advantages of implementing a strategy centered around AI-driven, agentic contract management. As a prominent figure in this field, CobbleStone Software is taking this opportunity to educate attendees on the benefits of this innovative approach.

data.title: How A Crypto Journalist Fell For A Sophisticated North Korean Phishing Attack: 'Giving Me Scam Vibes'
data.description: A journalist from Fortune, Ben Weiss, was recently targeted in a sophisticated phishing attack, believed to be carried out by North Korean hackers. He was lured into a trap in mid-March through a deceptive message on Telegram, allegedly from a hedge fund investor. The message led him to a Zoom call with someone claiming to be a Bitcoin strategist named Adam Swick, who pitched a new digital asset treasury opportunity. Despite reservations, Weiss agreed to the call through a Zoom link provided on Telegram. However, the link led to a fake Zoom interface with minor discrepancies and audio issues. To fix these problems, Weiss was prompted to download an update. The incident highlights the growing threat of cyber attacks, especially in sectors like journalism. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cox Mobile Announces $25K Grand Prize and Fan Favorite Winners of Phone Case Design Contest
data.description: Two teenagers from Boys & Girls Clubs in Virginia and Oklahoma have been selected as the recipients of $25,000 scholarships from Cox Mobile, the mobile phone service of Cox Communications. This announcement came as part of the second annual Phone Case Design competition. In addition to the scholarships awarded to the teens, the Boys & Girls Clubs in these states also received technology grants from Cox Mobile. This initiative by Cox Mobile aims to support and empower young individuals with educational opportunities while also benefiting the clubs through technology enhancements.

data.title: Insight Health Raises $11M Series A Led by Standard Capital to Fix Healthcare’s Administrative Crisis
data.description: Insight Health, a clinical agent platform for healthcare, revealed today that it has secured $11 million in a Series A funding round, spearheaded by Standard Capital. Other contributors include Pear VC, Kindred Ventures, Eudemian, ElevenLabs, and 43. With a trillion-dollar administrative crisis looming over the U.S. healthcare system, patient access to medical care remains notably low despite soaring costs. Administrative overhead has now surged past $1 trillion per year, burdening clinics and medical practices.

data.title: Tesla Deliveries: Elon Musk 'Actively Sacrificing' EV Business For AI
data.description: Elon Musk's Tesla reported first-quarter delivery figures that fell short of estimates, causing concern among some investors. While critics saw this as validation of their doubts, supporters attributed it to challenges faced in transitioning towards a self-driving car era. Despite missing expectations, Tesla's deliveries remain a crucial part of its business strategy. Musk's focus on advancing artificial intelligence technology has led some to believe he is prioritizing AI development over the electric vehicle division.

data.title: CFTC Sues Illinois, Arizona, Connecticut Over Prediction Market Oversight
data.description: On Thursday, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) filed lawsuits against Illinois, Arizona, and Connecticut for their efforts to regulate prediction markets. This legal dispute revolves around conflicting jurisdictions. The CFTC's actions are in response to the states' attempt to oversee prediction market activities. The news was reported by Investor's Business Daily.
